reason_of_clustering: These entities are related as they represent different monthly futures for live cattle. Investors and analysts track these contracts collectively to understand price trends and supply-demand dynamics over time.

Similar to the live cattle futures, these feeder cattle contracts are time-related and are tracked together. Analysts compare these months to gain insights into feeder cattle market trends and price shifts leading up to the feeding period.

This cluster encompasses both live and feeder cattle futures for the same time periods, allowing for a direct comparison of price performance and trends between different segments of the cattle market, which is critical for investment decisions.
